content: The inbox uses a pending/done directory structure: `.pi/inbox/pending/` for new messages and `.pi/inbox/done/` for processed messages. Files are moved atomically from pending to done after processing — no rewriting, no flag scanning. The directory location is the status, so the `processed` field can be dropped.

provenance.raw_agent: Here's how I'd structure it: .pi/inbox/ pending/ ← new messages land here, done/ ← processor moves them here after handling. The processor can: List pending/, Pick up a file, Process it, mv pending/{file} done/{file} — atomic, no rewriting, no flag scanning.
provenance.raw_user: I think pending and done might be best
provenance.what_was_ratified: pending/done directory structure for the inbox with atomic file moves instead of in-file processed flags
